/cwe/weakness/346.Name: Origin Validation Error
/cwe/weakness/346.Description: The product does not properly verify that the source of data or communication is valid.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: REALIZATION: This weakness is caused during implementation of an architectural security tactic.

CommonConsequences.Note: An attacker can access any functionality that is inadvertently accessible to the source.

Entries.IntroText: This Android application will remove a user account when it receives an intent to do so:

Entries.BodyText: This application does not check the origin of the intent, thus allowing any malicious application to remove a user. Always check the origin of an intent, or create an allowlist of trusted applications using the manifest.xml file.

Entries.IntroText: These Android and iOS applications intercept URL loading within a WebView and perform special actions if a particular URL scheme is used, thus allowing the Javascript within the WebView to communicate with the application:

Entries.BodyText: A call into native code can then be initiated by passing parameters within the URL:
Because the application does not check the source, a malicious website loaded within this WebView has the same access to the API as a trusted site.
